name: okta_anonymizing_vpn_login
description: A user signed in through an anonymizing proxy/VPN (user.session.start with isProxy true).

validation.notes: Previously not ported because okta.security.is_proxy was not ingested. It is now present (boolean), so the core Panther rule (eventType == user.session.start AND securityContext.isProxy) ports 1:1. DEGRADED: the Panther severity logic demotes Apple Private Relay to INFO via p_enrichment.ipinfo_privacy, which Honeycomb does not ingest; all matches surface at the same severity here. HIGH VOLUME / TUNING: 493 hits/90d are dominated by legitimate corporate VPN/CDN egress (Fastly, Cloudflare, Google One VPN, office networks). For a usable alert, pair with okta.debug.risk CONTAINS "Anonymizing Proxy" or risk level HIGH, exclude known-good ASNs (net.peer.as.org / net.peer.as.number), or treat this as an enrichment signal rather than a standalone page. net.peer.as.org, net.peer.domain, and net.peer.isp are surfaced as context in place of the Panther asOrg/isp/domain title fields.
